items.title: Pengerang Energy Complex appoints China's CNCEC as EPCC contractor
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Pengerang Energy Complex Sdn Bhd (PEC) has awarded the engineering, procurement, construction and commissioning (EPCC) contract for its downstream petrochemical facility in Johor to China National Chemical Engineering Co Ltd (CNCEC). PEC said CNCEC was selected following a comprehensive evaluation process that assessed technical expertise, financial strength and project execution capabilities. The company cited CNCEC's experience in delivering large-scale energy and petrochemical projects, as well as its familiarity with Honeywell UOP technologies that form part of the project's process design.

items.title: Man found dead outside HTAA believed to have fallen from hospital building
items.summary: KUANTAN: A man found unconscious in front of the main lobby of Tengku Ampuan Afzan Hospital (HTAA) on June 6 is believed to have fallen from the hospital building. "Investigations found that the victim had previously received treatment for mental health issues at the hospital. On June 6, the Pahang Health Department said the man was found lying unconscious outside the hospital building about 10am before medical personnel confirmed his death.

items.title: Chow non-committal on fresh extension for Jelutong reclamation project
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow remains non-committal as to whether Penang will grant another extension for the controversial Jelutong reclamation project, citing an ongoing review. ProtectKarpal has been actively campaigning against the project. Chow said the state's consideration of the environmental impact assessment (EIA) application remains ongoing and that no decision has been reached.

items.title: Metal‑cutting caused former Odeon Cinema blaze in Penang that killed Myanmar worker, probe finds
items.summary: Penang Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) director Mohamad Shoki Hamzah said forensic investigations found the fire was accidentally triggered during renovation works at the premises. “CCTV footage showed two of the three workers carrying out metal cutting work using an oxy-cutter set before hot molten metal residue fell onto combustible materials below and ignited a fire. Mohamad Shoki said inspections by the department’s K9 Detection Unit found no traces of fire accelerants at the scene.

items.title: PN may be 'put to sleep' if divisions persist, say analysts
items.summary: Universiti Malaya political analyst Dr Mohammad Tawfik Yaakub said PN is no longer sustainable without both Pas and Bersatu, but the latest split is likely to spark a contest between the two parties to determine who ultimately controls the PN brand. He said Bersatu would be at greater risk if it failed to remain within PN, as the coalition has largely depended on Pas' grassroots machinery and electoral strength. "This means Pas may leave PN, while Muhyiddin could continue to lead PN with Bersatu, Gerakan and other component parties."

items.title: Good drivers can get 10pct insurance premium discount - Loke
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Motorists with good driving records can now enjoy insurance premium discounts of up to 10 per cent under the newly launched Cermat Madani programme. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the initiative, introduced by the Transport Ministry in collaboration with 10 insurance and takaful providers, rewards drivers with low-risk profiles based on their driving records and traffic summonses. Motorists renewing their road tax through the MyJPJ application would undergo a risk assessment by participating insurers and takaful operators.

items.title: Saifuddin: Any alliance with Pas ultimately ends in split
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: Pas' decision to cut ties with Bersatu is nothing new as the party had previously severed relationships with other parties and coalitions, says Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail. The Pakatan Harapan secretary-general said the decision made by the Pas central working committee last night was a mere repeat of a long-standing pattern in national politics. He said every political cooperation entered into by Pas would inevitably end in a split.

items.title: 18 reports lodged over reckless Triton driver who damaged vehicles on NSE
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM: Police have received 18 reports from motorists whose vehicles were damaged after being struck by a Mitsubishi Triton driven recklessly along the North-South Expressway (NSE) near here yesterday. Penang police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said preliminary investigations showed that 18 vehicles were damaged in a series of collisions involving the 29-year-old driver. He said the suspect had been remanded for four days from today to assist investigations.

items.title: Automatic citizenship policy for children born overseas to Malaysian mothers 'in final stages'
items.summary: Pix/ Video Alias Abd Rani BATU PAHAT: The Home Ministry is in the final stages of implementing a policy to grant automatic citizenship to children born overseas to Malaysian mothers married to foreign spouses. Under the new policy, the ministry will grant citizenship by operation of law, meaning Malaysian mothers will no longer have to go through lengthy processes to secure legal status for their children. Previously, children born overseas to Malaysian mothers married to foreigners were required to apply for citizenship.

items.title: Sarawak Water: Tests tonight to trace major leak and determine whether 20,000 or 70,000 Miri consumers face water outage
items.summary: Sarawak Water acting chief executive officer Malseni Jamal said the assessment would involve temporarily shutting off a 750mm valve to establish whether the leak originates from the 750mm pipeline or the larger 1.1 metre pipeline. Malseni said if the leak is traced to the 750mm pipeline, the areas affected would be Taman Jelita, Taman Tunku, Jalan Engkabang, Sungai Rait, Eastwood, Hilltop, and Riam. Other affected areas would include Kampung Beraya, Kuala Sibuti, Bukit Song, Sungai Karab, Bukit Peninjau, Beluru town area, and Sungai Seruas.

items.title: Penang revenue surge driven by surplus, not tax hikes, says Chow
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Penang's rise in state revenue to more than RM600 million was driven by a large budget surplus in 2025 and not by increases in taxes or charges imposed on the people, says Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow. Chow was responding to critics who questioned the state's financial position after he said Penang's consolidated revenue account had surpassed RM600 million and was on track towards the RM1 billion mark. Yesterday, Chow said Penang's consolidated revenue reserves had rebounded to RM616 million, dismissing claims that the state was heading towards bankruptcy due to dwindling funds.

items.title: Temporary relief centre opened for 110 Kampung Gok Kapur fire victims
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The state Welfare Department has opened a temporary relief centre to accommodate 110 people from 29 families whose homes were destroyed in a fire at Kampung Cina, here yesterday. Its director, Che Samsuzuki Che Noh, said the centre was opened at Mukim Banggol mosque about 6.30pm yesterday. Che Samsuzuki said the department had also distributed emergency assistance kits to the affected victims.

items.title: Penang CM says rise in state reserves predates quit rent increase
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 9 — Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow today dismissed claims that the state’s cash reserves had risen to more than RM600 million because of the recent quit rent hike, saying the increase was driven by a surplus recorded in 2025. He said the balance in the state’s Consolidated Revenue Account had already increased before the revised quit rent rates came into effect on January 1 this year. Chow said the state recorded a surplus in 2025, with cash reserves exceeding RM500 million as of December 31, 2025.

items.title: Budget 2027 to stay development‑driven amid crisis‑year pressures, says Akmal Nasrullah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 9 — The Ministry of Economy will continue to focus on development expenditure in Budget 2027, which is expected to be tabled this October, said Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir. He said all ministries have submitted their development proposals, and evaluations are being conducted at the ministry level through a bidding mechanism. Akmal Nasrullah said a detailed review of those development plans will begin next month.

items.title: PN turmoil exposes Samsuri's leadership failure, says Bersatu leader
items.summary: SEREMBAN: The ongoing conflict within Perikatan Nasional (PN) is seen as a failure of Pas vice-president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar in his role as coalition chairman. "Samsuri has also been relatively quiet in recent weeks on important matters, including political developments in Negri Sembilan, rakyat issues and internal tensions within PN. "Samsuri's position as Pas vice-president has limited his ability to act independently as PN chairman, contributing to Pas' decision to end its political cooperation with Bersatu, as announced by the party's central leadership last night," he said in a statement today.

items.title: Fitch cuts 2026 global growth forecast to 2.4pct on oil shock, raises China outlook
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Fitch Ratings has lowered its 2026 global growth forecast to 2.4 per cent from 2.6 per cent previously, citing the economic impact of the oil crisis triggered by the US-Iran war and the prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz. The ratings agency cut its 2026 growth projections for the United States and eurozone by 0.3 and 0.4 percentage points respectively, to 1.9 per cent and 0.9 per cent. Growth for emerging markets excluding China was also revised lower to 3.2 per cent.

items.title: Police: 'Foreigners' flat' in Hulu Langat raided last year, 42 illegals detained
items.summary: https://www.facebook.com/share/r/18YQWPkDKY/ KUALA LUMPUR: Police have clarified that a structure allegedly built illegally by foreigners in Hulu Langat, which recently went viral on social media, was raided last year, with 42 undocumented migrants detained. Kajang police chief Assistant Commissioner Naazron Abdul Yusof said the clarification followed the circulation of a 47-second Facebook video on Tuesday showing a structure in Kampung Kenangan, Sungai Makau, allegedly erected illegally by foreigners. He said police and the Selangor Immigration Department conducted an integrated enforcement operation, known as Ops Kutip, at the location on Feb 28, 2025.

items.title: Khazanah CIO: Malaysia must move beyond manufacturing
items.summary: Khazanah Nasional Bhd chief investment officer Datuk Hisham Hamdan said Malaysia was among the countries that benefited when markets sought for competitive destinations for manufacturing capital and production across Asia when the Japanese yen appreciated in 1985. As manufacturing activities increasingly concentrated in China, Malaysia's competitive advantage based on cost alone became harder to sustain. "The divergence reflects a deeper challenge in Malaysia's productive capabilities," he said in the Khazanah Report 2025.

items.title: Chow: Penang seeking DoE input before deciding on Jelutong landfill rehabilitation project
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 9 — The Penang government has met the Department of Environment (DoE) to better understand the environmental impact assessment (EIA) considerations for the proposed Jelutong landfill rehabilitation and Karpal Singh Drive reclamation project, Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said today. Chow said the state government’s decision-making process must be guided by verified information and proper procedures. He said the state government must follow the proper process as any decision would have implications, particularly financial ones.

items.title: BN-PH ties unchanged despite Johor contest strategy, says Zahid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi says the coalition's decision to contest the Johor election independently does not signal any change in its relationship with Pakatan Harapan (PH). Zahid said state politics should not be read as a reflection of national trends, citing differing circumstances in Johor, Melaka, Pahang and Perak. Asked whether BN's decision in Johor suggested a potential shift in national cooperation, he said it was normal for parties to contest independently at state level.

items.title: Mukah police seek public help to find two 14‑year‑old girls missing since Sunday
items.summary: MUKAH, June 9 — Police are seeking help from the public to locate two 14-year-old schoolgirls who were reported missing yesterday. Mukah police chief Muhamad Rizal Alias said Easter Agnes David and Frocilea Rajok Jessica were last seen in Mukah town on Sunday at around 1.30pm. “The two missing girls are identified as Easter Agnes David, an Iban girl from Rumah Jali at Batu 15, Jalan Selangau-Mukah, while Frocilea Rajok Jessica is from Rumah Nelson Chuat Begeri, KM59 Jalan Mukah-Selangau,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Golden Land, Maple Hospitality to introduce MapleSuites brand in Sabah project
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Golden Land Bhd (GLB) has signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with Maple Hospitality Group (MHG), introducing the MapleSuites brand in Sabah through its upcoming project, The Playground @ Kota Kinabalu. The partnership marks a significant milestone for both companies, combining GLB's aspirational development vision with MHG's hospitality management expertise to create an integrated hospitality ecosystem in Kota Kinabalu while enhancing the long-term value proposition of GLB's Sabah assets. "The Playground @ Kota Kinabalu reflects our aspiration to create a development that goes beyond conventional real estate by integrating culture, lifestyle, hospitality, and long-term value creation," said Stewart Yap, chief executive officer of GLB.

items.title: Tuaran security guard pleads guilty to sexually assaulting 11‑year‑old girl, sentencing set for June 11
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 9 — The Sessions Court here will sentence a security guard, who pleaded guilty to physically sexually assaulting a minor, on June 11. Judge Amir Shah Amir Hassan adjourned the sentencing of Mohd Firdaus Basir, 34, at the request of the prosecution. Mohd Firdaus said every human makes mistakes and appealed for leniency, calling on the court not to impose heavier punishments.

items.title: Karangkraf delegation tours Media Prima HQ, reaffirms industry cooperation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A delegation from Karangkraf Media Group today visited Media Prima Bhd's new headquarters in Anjung Liku, Balai Berita, Bangsar, to strengthen ties between two of Malaysia's leading media organisations. The delegation was led by Karangkraf Group chairman Firdaus Hussamuddin and included Sinar Karangkraf chief executive officer Farah Hussamuddin, Karangkraf Group executive director Akmal Eirfan Fauzi, Ultimate Print chief executive officer Azizi Fickry, and senior representatives from Sinar Karangkraf, Sinar Harian, Sinar Daily, Nu Ideaktiv, Buku Karangkraf and Ultimate Print. They were received by Media Prima group managing director Datuk Rafiq Razali, group chief financial officer Rosli Sabarudin, Media Prima Audio chief executive officer Nazri Noran, Media Prima News and Current Affairs Division group managing editor Jasbant Singh, and other members of the company's senior management team.

items.title: NGOs raise alarm over rising hate speech against Rohingya refugees
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A coalition of civil society organisations has expressed alarm over what it described as a growing wave of hate speech, misinformation and intimidation targeting the Rohingya refugee community in Malaysia. The groups said false narratives circulating on social media, including claims that Rohingya refugees are demanding citizenship, political power or territorial autonomy, have been deliberately amplified to incite fear and hostility. Others include Sarawak Women for Women, Family Frontiers, Pusat Komas, Bersih, Freedom Film Network, the Socialist Party of Malaysia, Iman Research, Asylum Access Malaysia and the Rohingya Maìyafuìnor Collaborative Network.

items.title: Putrajaya urged to tax sweetened condensed milk to curb NCD crisis
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Putrajaya should introduce a specific excise tax on sweetened condensed milk and sweetened creamer, expanding the national "War against Sugar" campaign under the 2027 Budget. Health think-tank Galen Centre for Health and Social Policy chief executive officer Azrul Mohd Khalib said the existing sugar-sweetened beverage tax alone was insufficient to address the country's growing non-communicable diseases (NCD) crisis. "The Galen Centre proposes that the government introduce a tiered excise tax on sweetened condensed milk, sweetened creamer and similar high-sugar milk-based products, based on sugar content per 100g," he said in a statement today.

items.title: Jaslinda's going home: Rescued hiker discharged from hospital
items.summary: TAPAH: Hiker Jaslinda Saludin, 49, who was found safe after spending two weeks lost on Gunung Batu Putih, was discharged from Tapah Hospital today after three days of treatment. Using a walking stick and with her left leg bandaged, Jaslinda thanked Malaysians for their prayers and those involved in the search and rescue operation. Jaslinda also apologised to search personnel and others involved in efforts to locate her.

items.title: Alibaba Cloud launches Johor cloud region, boosts Malaysia AI push
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Alibaba Cloud has launched a new public cloud region in Johor, supported by two new data centres as it continues to strengthen its cloud and artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure in Malaysia amid growing demand for digital and AI services. The expansions form part of the company's previously announced US$53 billion investment in AI and cloud infrastructure and bring Alibaba Cloud's global network to 104 availability zones across 32 regions. Several Malaysian companies are already leveraging Alibaba Cloud's infrastructure and AI technologies.

items.title: Missile maker Kongsberg seeks talks with Malaysia over NSM dispute
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Norwegian defence firm Kongsberg Defence and Aerospace (KDA) has sought discussions with Malaysia over the latter's compensation claims following the collapse of the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) procurement deal. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the ministry had received a request from the company, but stressed that Malaysia would continue pursuing its claims over Kongsberg's failure to fulfil the contract. However, our claims (for compensation) are still ongoing.

items.title: 'Short, sharp and more strategic': EU's new approach to climate talks
items.summary: THE EU wants to target fewer, clearer goals at the world's annual climate summit in November, building alliances in advance to avoid a repeat of last year's bruising talks where the bloc struggled to advance its agenda, a document showed on Monday. The EU's negotiating mandate should be "shorter, sharper and more strategic" than in previous years, the document said. But ambition alone is not a strategy," the document said.

items.title: Indonesian rupiah strengthens after surprise BI rate hike
items.summary: The Jakarta Composite Index was up 4.8 per cent at 5,599.74 points prior to the midday break. The benchmark rose as much as 5.3 per cent earlier in the day after tumbling around 4.5 per cent in the previous session. The MSCI EM Asia equities index jumped four per cent, reversing much of the decline from Monday.

items.title: 'Several actions' to follow after Umno-Pas talks, says Zahid
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said several actions should follow recent talks between his party and Pas. Zahid, who is also deputy prime minister, said the meeting, which he described as a positive step, focused on resolving non-political matters. He said any political matters arising from the discussions would be refined in line with Umno's commitment to the unity government.

items.title: Pahang Sultan opens first Islamic religious primary school in Cameron Highlands
items.summary: CAMERON HIGHLANDS, June 9 — Sultan of Pahang Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri’ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah today officially opened the first Pahang Islamic Religious Primary School (SRAIN) at its temporary premises in Golden Hill, Tanah Rata, Cameron Highlands. His Royal Highness arrived at the school at 10.20 am and was received by Pahang Islamic Religious Department (JAIP) director Datuk Ahmad Khirrizal Ab Rahman. “Our goal is to have a Pahang Islamic Religious Primary School in every district, managed directly by JAIP,” he said.

items.title: Teacher claims trial to five counts of sexually assaulting student, two pornography charges
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: A teacher was charged in two Sessions Courts and a magistrate's court here today with five counts of physical sexual assault against his female student and two counts of possessing pornography. The accused, Mohamad Amirul Firdaus Awang Meran, 28, pleaded not guilty to all charges. The accused pleaded not guilty before Sessions Court judge Nor Aziati Jaafar to three charges of physical sexual assault against an 11-year-old girl while holding a position of trust as her teacher.

items.title: Man jailed three months for stealing KTMB track cables in Klang
items.summary: KLANG, June 9 — The Magistrate’s Court here today sentenced a 35-year-old man to three months’ imprisonment after he pleaded guilty to stealing track cables belonging to Keretapi Tanah Melayu Berhad (KTMB) three days ago. Magistrate Nor Azilah Mat handed down the sentence on Railey Nasal and ordered that it take effect from the date of his arrest. Railey was charged with stealing a 40-metre-long 16-millimetre track cable and a 20-metre-long 2.5-millimetre track cable belonging to KTMB, with an estimated value totalling RM850, from the KTM railway track near Jalan Sembilang 8E, Jalan Telok Pulai, at about 1.20 pm on June 6.

items.title: Nine witnesses questioned in alleged slap case involving uni student, politician
items.summary: KUANTAN: Pahang police have recorded statements from nine individuals as part of investigations into a university student's claim that he was slapped by a senator in Bentong on May 20. Pahang police chief Datuk Seri Yahaya Othman said statements had been recorded from witnesses, including the politician and the 23-year-old victim, who is the grandson of former Kelantan menteri besar Tan Sri Nik Abdul Aziz Nik Mat. Both the suspect, and the victim, who did not suffer serious injuries, lodged separate police reports.

items.title: Philippines takes diplomatic action against China over floating structure in South China Sea
items.summary: MANILA: The Philippines' foreign ministry has undertaken appropriate diplomatic action against China in connection with the "illegal presence" of a floating structure in a disputed atoll, the country's South China Sea task force said on Tuesday. The task force confirmed the presence of a floating structure measuring six by six metres and featuring what appeared to be an antenna in the disputed Scarborough Shoal in the South China Sea. The structure, initially spotted at the entrance to the shoal and later inside its lagoon, appeared to be manned, the task force said, citing the latest images from the armed forces.

items.title: Indonesia raises rates in surprise move to support sinking rupiah
items.summary: JAKARTA: Indonesia's central bank raised interest rates by 25 basis points on Tuesday in an unexpected off-cycle rate hike, taking the benchmark rate to 5.50 per cent as it moves to stabilise the rupiah. Bank Indonesia (BI) said in a statement posted on its website that the off-cycle rate hike was necessary because "the rupiah exchange rate has weakened more than expected" since its last meeting. The rupiah has been under mounting pressure for weeks, and is one of the world's worst performing currencies, falling 8 per cent this year and 7 per cent since the Iran war erupted.

items.title: Zahid: Pas–Bersatu split shows alliances need sincerity, not convenience
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Umno had anticipated Pas' decision to end its cooperation with Bersatu, with party president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi describing the split as a lesson that political alliances must be built on sincerity rather than convenience. Zahid, who is also deputy prime minister, said political cooperation could not be sustained by calculations alone and must be anchored in a long-term vision for national unity and development. Zahid also issued a veiled warning over any future political arrangements arising from the split, saying commitments must go beyond symbolic gestures.

items.title: Malaysia must strengthen fiscal discipline, improve service delivery to attract high‑value investment, says Amir Hamzah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 9 — Malaysia must continue to strengthen fiscal discipline, improve public service delivery, support businesses, protect the rakyat and position the country to capture higher-value investment and growth, said Finance Minister II Senator Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan. He said these would ensure that reform is anchored in credibility, accountability and trust. Amir Hamzah said this in his keynote address at the Malaysia Institute of Accountants (MIA) International Accountants Conference 2026 here today.

items.title: Zahid: Kemas children to move seamlessly into national preschools, primary education under new MoU
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 9 – Rural and Regional Development Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi today said a new early childhood education pathway linking Kemas childcare and kindergartens to national preschool and primary education is being developed through a strategic collaboration between the Rural and Regional Development Ministry (KKDW) and the Education Ministry (MoE). He said he initiated efforts to position Tabika under Kemas as “feeder kindergartens” for preschools in national schools across the country. “Education for children under the age of five will be under Kemas, while preschool education at age five will be under the Education Ministry.

items.title: Bukit Aman: BTS concert ticket scams leave fans RM95,000 poorer
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 9 — The Bukit Aman Commercial Crime Investigation Department (CCID) has advised the public to remain vigilant against concert ticket scams involving BTS World Tour that have been detected across various social media platforms. CCID director Comm Datuk Rusdi Mohd Isa said authorities have detected syndicates and scammers using various online platforms to offer concert tickets, particularly for VIP, Soundcheck and Priority Access categories. “Avoid making payments to individuals offering tickets via social media without verified credentials; and be cautious of claims involving special access, insider allocations or pre-sale tickets before official sales open.

items.title: Over 2.7 million eligible to vote in Johor polls, Election Commission to appoint 42,000 workers
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 9 — The Election Commission (EC) has registered over 2.7 million electors eligible to cast their ballots in the 16th Johor state election, based on the Supplementary Electoral Roll for April 2026, which was gazetted on May 21. He said that approximately 42,000 election workers are expected to be appointed and mobilised to manage all aspects of the state election. The workforce will include 56 Returning Officers, election personnel and security officers involved throughout the election period.

items.title: ‘Just don’t blame Umno’: Khaled on PAS-Bersatu split
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 9 — Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin today said the party had no hand in the Islamist party Pas’ decision to end cooperation with Bersatu. PAS president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang announced the decision to terminate political cooperation with Bersatu last night following weeks of internal tussle in the Perikatan Nasional (PN) coalition. Despite terminating political cooperation with Bersatu, PAS is reportedly remaining in the PN camp.

items.title: Muhyiddin: Bersatu staying put in Perikatan despite PAS ending cooperation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 9 — Parti Pribumi Bersatu today said it will remain in Perikatan Nasional (PN) despite PAS’ decision to end political cooperation between the two parties. In a statement, Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin said the party would continue strengthening the opposition coalition as one of its founding members. He said the decision to end political cooperation and sever ties with Bersatu was made by PAS.

items.title: Johor primary school teacher claims trial to sexual offences involving pupil, possession of obscene material
items.summary: The accused, Mohamad Amirul Awang Meran, who has been teaching for five years, also claimed trial in the Magistrates’ Court to two counts of possessing obscene material found on his mobile phones. The accused, who is attached to a primary school in Masai, entered his plea after the charges were read before three judicial officers — Judge Nor Aziati Jaafar, Judge Madihah Zainol and Magistrate Afifah Hazimah Wahab. Judge Nor Aziati set bail at RM10,000, Judge Madihah set bail at RM3,000, and Magistrate Afifah Hazimah set bail at RM6,000.

items.title: Zahid: Umno-PAS talks open constructive space for non-political issues
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 9 — Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi described the recent discussions between the leadership of Umno and PAS as a positive step towards addressing various issues, including matters not strictly related to politics. He said such engagements should be seen as part of ongoing efforts to strengthen dialogue and explore new approaches amid evolving political dynamics. “We welcome the meeting held between the leadership of Umno and PAS to discuss and resolve several issues and agendas that are not necessarily political in nature.

items.title: Zahid: PAS-Bersatu split was expected amid shifting political trends
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 9 — Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the fallout between PAS and Bersatu was not entirely unexpected, describing it as a development that had been anticipated based on prevailing political trends. Ahmad Zahid said any form of political cooperation must be built on sincerity, trust and a genuine commitment to nation-building rather than short-term political considerations. Ahmad Zahid said political alliances should not be viewed solely through the lens of electoral advantage, but should instead serve broader national interests.

items.title: Malaysia to pursue compensation over cancelled missile deal as Norwegian firm seeks talks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 9 — Norwegian defence firm Kongsberg Defence & Aerospace is seeking to negotiate with Malaysia after the latter pressed Norway to expedite compensation for a scrapped missile procurement deal. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin confirmed the matter but stressed that Malaysia will continue pursuing the compensation concurrently despite any possible negotiations with the company. Kongsberg Defence & Aerospace signed a contract with the Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN) in April 2018 to supply naval strike missiles (NSM) for Malaysia’s upcoming littoral combat ships (LCS).

items.title: Viral Mitsubishi Triton driver remanded four days over reckless driving and drug-related offences
items.summary: SEBERANG PERAI, June 9 — A four-wheel-drive driver arrested for reckless and dangerous driving has been remanded for four days until June 12 to assist investigations. Seberang Perai Tengah district police chief Assistant Commissioner Nik Aminuddin Raja Abdullah said police received information about a road incident involving the vehicle before officers were deployed to the scene. Nik Aminuddin said upon further inspection, police found substances believed to be drugs in a sling bag the suspect was carrying.

items.title: Three littoral mission vessels set for delivery by end-2027, says defence minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 9 — All three Littoral Mission Ship Batch 2 (LMSB2) vessels currently being constructed in Turkiye are on track for delivery by end-2027, Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said today. Mohamed Khaled said the second LMSB2 vessel was named and launched at the Istanbul Shipyard on Sunday, following the maiden vessel’s launch on May 24, 2026. The procurement of the three LMSB2 vessels reportedly costs €536.5 million (RM2.5 billion).

items.title: Malaysia keeps 2026 OPEC production quota unchanged, says Akmal Nasrullah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 9 — Malaysia will maintain its petroleum production quota under the Organisation of the Petroleum Exporting Countries and its allies (OPEC+) for 2026, as previously agreed, Economy Minister Datuk Seri Akmal Nasrullah Ahmad Nasir said. He added that the government has agreed to cooperate with the study to ensure Malaysia maintains a balanced quota that is aligned with its production capacity, noting that a suitable quota for Malaysia in 2027 remains under review as the country’s petroleum output fluctuates according to operational conditions. Therefore, the quota to be set for 2027 must reflect the actual production capacity of our petroleum sector,” he added.

items.title: Fuel subsidy commitment helps curb inflation and protect rakyat, says Fomca
items.summary: He said maintaining fuel prices at current levels greatly helps reduce transportation and logistics cost pressures, while also supporting the supply chain to prevent increases in the prices of daily necessities. “However, Fomca is of the view that fuel prices are not the only factor determining the prices of goods. “Consumers still face challenges from food costs, rental rates, energy costs, wages and various other operating expenses that also influence market prices.

items.title: Collins Aerospace expands Subang facility with RM256m investment to boost Malaysia’s MRO ambitions, says Anthony Loke
items.summary: SUBANG, June 9 — Collins Aerospace today announced a US$63 million (RM256 million) expansion of its maintenance, repair and overhaul (MRO) facility at Subang Aerotech Park, quadrupling its footprint and strengthening Malaysia’s ambitions to become a leading aerospace servicing hub in the Asia-Pacific region. Officiating the expansion today, Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the investment reflected growing confidence in Malaysia as a destination for high-value aerospace investments. “Collins Aerospace’s US$63 million facility expansion here is a statement of confidence in Malaysia’s infrastructure, confidence in our regulatory environment, and above all, confidence in our people,” he said in his speech.

items.title: Two women killed after being thrown from Myvi in Sarawak crash with stalled lorry on Batang Sadong Bridge
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 9 — Two women were killed early this morning after they were thrown from a Perodua Myvi during a collision involving a stalled lorry at the Batang Sadong Bridge in Simunjan, Sarawak. The Myvi driver, identified as 30-year-old Harudin Ramli, sustained injuries and was taken to hospital for treatment. The lorry driver, 22-year-old Wan Aidiladha Wan Hadani, was unhurt.

items.title: Viral video leads police to five suspects in TM cable theft case involving luxury cars in TTDI
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 9 — Police are tracking down five men suspected of stealing Telekom Malaysia ™ cables in Taman Tun Dr Ismail (TTDI) here yesterday, after the incident, which involved several luxury vehicles, was captured on video and went viral on social media. Brickfields police chief ACP Hoo Chang Hook said police became aware of the incident through the viral footage and immediately launched an investigation. Those with relevant information may contact the nearest police station or the Brickfields District Police Headquarters (IPD).

items.title: AI development must be anchored in human values, not just efficiency, Anwar says in Tokyo lecture
items.summary: TOKYO, June 9 — The development of artificial intelligence must be grounded in human values, moral responsibility and integrity, rather than driven purely by technological efficiency or economic gain, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today. He said while Malaysia supports technological development, including AI, the growing integration of such systems into homes, schools, hospitals, workplaces, public institutions and even human imagination poses significant challenges. “We support technological progress, including AI.

items.title: Majority of Malaysian workers earn RM5,000 and below as cost pressures mount
items.summary: Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the figures underline how deeply inflation is weighing on household finances, particularly in urban centres where expenses continue to climb faster than wages. A typical young household in Kuala Lumpur with two small children would require about RM6,183 a month just to meet essential living costs, based on the Basic Expenditure for Decent Living (PAKW) benchmark. Separately, Universiti Sains Islam Malaysia economics lecturer Prof Dr Nuradli Ridzwan Shah Mohd Dali said around 80 per cent of Malaysians are effectively living from paycheque to paycheque, with little financial buffer.

items.title: Former MACC chief Azam Baki files defamation suit against businessman Albert Tei
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 9 — Former Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki has filed a defamation suit against businessman Albert Tei over statements published on social media and online platforms. In a statement today, law firm Zain Megat & Murad, which represents Azam, said a writ of summons and statement of claim were filed at the Shah Alam High Court yesterday, according to a report published by Berita Harian . Tei had recently made several allegations against Azam in relation to disputes and ongoing investigations.

items.title: Kelantan MB assures aid, shelter for Kampung Gok Kapor fire victims
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, June 9 — Kelantan Menteri Besar Datuk Mohd Nassuruddin Daud has assured victims of the Kampung Gok Kapor fire here that they will receive proper shelter, assistance and support. Mohd Nassuruddin said he received news of the fire in the Kijang state constituency shortly after arriving at Kuala Lumpur International Airport. Yesterday, 27 houses were destroyed in the fire at Kampung Gok Kapor.
